title: malus × floribunda
intro: Malus × floribunda, also known as the flowering crabapple, is a cross between the domesticated apple (Malus domestica) and one of the Asian crabapples (Malus spp.). This plant is valued for its fragrant flowers, edible fruits, and medicinal properties. The bark, leaves, and flowers of Malus × floribunda have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and inflammation, due to their antiseptic, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations from Malus × floribunda, such as infusions and tinctures, have been used to promote respiratory health, reduce fever, and soothe sore throats, among other benefits.
